intro: Knema sumatrana, also known as Sumatran canarium, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Knema sumatrana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been found to possess antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. In herbal medicine, Knema sumatrana is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in the form of capsules or tinctures to exploit its purported health benefits.
